scenario: Helix Insurance plans to use start instructions in a Camunda 8.8 integration test that processes 800 claims per night. A developer asks whether the input mappings declared on the targeted element are honored when an instruction starts the instance at that element of the process model.
question: What is the documented default behavior for input mappings when a startInstructions entry targets an element?

options.text: The instruction starts before the targeted element by default, so the input mappings of that element are applied as usual when it is activated on creation.
The instruction starts after the targeted element by default, so the input mappings of that element are skipped and only output mappings run on its completion.
The instruction toggles input mappings off for the targeted element, requiring teams to redeclare them as a separate runtime instruction on the same command.
The instruction routes through the none start event first, so input mappings on the targeted element are applied twice during creation of the process instance.

a.text: Correct. By default a startInstructions entry starts before the given element, which means the input mappings of that element are applied as usual when the engine activates it on creation.

b.text: Incorrect. The default position is before the element, not after; input mappings are applied as usual rather than skipped on activation.

c.text: Incorrect. Start instructions do not toggle input mappings off and runtime instructions only describe where to terminate the instance, not how mappings run.

d.text: Incorrect. Start instructions activate the targeted element directly; there is no documented re-entry through the none start event that would re-apply input mappings.

explanation: A start instruction defaults to starting the process instance before the targeted element, so that element's input mappings are applied as usual when the engine activates it during process instance creation.
